name: Elegant Chestnut Soup
recipeInstructions:
To roast chestnuts: Select fresh, firm, unwrinkled chestnuts without any signs of mold. Make two small cuts in the flat side of each chestnut using a sharp knife (an X shape). Place the chestnuts, cut-side up, on a low baking sheet with a thin layer of water at the bottom. Bake for 20-25 minutes at 350°F until browned and the cuts peel back easily from the heat. Check one chestnut for tenderness before removing from the oven; allow it to cool slightly before peeling and using.
Prepare the chestnuts by removing the outer shell and fuzzy skin, leaving only the inner chestnut meat.
Heat the oil in a pan until hot, then sauté the onion until it becomes semi-tender. Add the celery and continue sautéing until both vegetables are very tender.
Combine the water and apple juice with the roasted chestnuts and all remaining ingredients. Mix well.
Simmer everything on low to medium heat (do not boil) for about 15-20 minutes, or until hot throughout and flavors have melded together.
Serve warm and garnish with chopped or ground nuts if desired. The soup can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 4-6 days.
